hero.positioning: Cyber and full-stack engineer. I build products to make my life easier, that run locally, without the cloud, and with AI.
hero.statusLine: > Seeking the perfect balance between being stimulated, usefull, and valued

projects.title: Projects
projects.subtitle: Local-first projects, built from end to end.

yui.pitch: AI voice assistant that runs the home, fully local and cloud-free.
yui.architecture: Wakeword detection feeds Whisper (STT); an LLM agent plans actions over a modular MCP tool layer; XTTS handles speech synthesis. The whole pipeline runs locally.
features: Local LLM agent with tool use
Modular MCP architecture for home control
XTTS voice synthesis, no cloud calls

hisho.pitch: A technical portfolio augmented by an LLM, turning free-form text into experiences, skills and CVs.
hisho.architecture: Nuxt 4 on the front, AdonisJS 6 and PostgreSQL on the back (Lucid models). Extraction runs through an OpenAI-compatible endpoint over SSE streaming: pasted text is turned into structured JSON (experiences, missions, skills), repaired if truncated, then imported idempotently. The model key stays server-side.

koya.pitch: A single interface to run the homelab: hosts, applications, deployments and nginx routes.
koya.architecture: Nuxt 4, AdonisJS 6 and PostgreSQL. Hosts and applications are stored in the database; the tool reads the nginx configs to infer existing zones and builds routes (proxy or redirect) toward a known host and port, telling apart local domains (.home.arpa, mkcert cert) from domains exposed through Cloudflare.

yoji.tagline: Local-first todos on top of Obsidian
yoji.pitch: Local-first todos on top of Obsidian, synced over Git. Your data stays yours.
yoji.architecture: A Nuxt app packaged for mobile with Capacitor. Tasks are Markdown checkboxes, read and written directly in the Obsidian vault; there's no database — the vault is the single source of truth. Syncing between devices goes through Git, with no central server.
features: Lives on top of your Obsidian vault

contact.title: Contact
contact.subtitle: A project, a question? Drop me a line.
contact.name: Name
contact.success: Thanks — your message has been sent.
contact.error: Something went wrong. Please try again.
contact.rateLimited: Too many attempts. Please wait a moment.

about: Passionate about science and a lifelong self-learner, I completed a cybersecurity engineering degree while running a web-development micro-business on the side.

Today I pair a DevSecOps mindset at work with active technical curiosity through maintaining my Homelab. This triptych (Code, Security, Hardware) gives me a holistic view of modern IT systems — and above all feeds my thirst to learn and build.
In short, a hybrid profile who can both quit Vim and center a div.

airbus.title: Cyber Engineer / Tech Lead - MCS Airbus Defence and Space
airbus.subtitle: On behalf of Neverhack
paragraphs: Through an innovative approach, I significantly optimized and improved many processes — x1400 in compute speed, +175% in accuracy, all while staying ahead of ESA's requirements. I quickly gained responsibility up to becoming technical lead, on fascinating projects such as building a vulnerability-management tool for European ground-segment systems (Egnos, Galileo, CO3D, PNEO...) to meet ESA's requirements.

I then designed a global security-audit solution covering an entire information system. The tool integrates vulnerability management and prioritization, obsolescence prevention and temporal analysis, ensuring a secure evolution of the system through reporting praised by Airbus.

Finally, I industrialized this solution into a full web platform (SaaS). Now accessible from every European site, it hosts many projects, makes daily work easier for thousands of Airbus employees and saves Airbus several million euros every year.

viveris.title: Full-stack Lead Dev - Viveris
paragraphs: I led a team of three junior web developers to design and build an application for organizing remote work across the company, with 3D visualization. The project was delivered end to end (from specification to release) using agile methods, making for the best possible first professional experience overall.

altab.title: Self-employed - Altab
paragraphs: During my studies, I built a micro-business under France's National Student-Entrepreneur Status. It let me launch many projects:
bullets: Full-stack + 3D development of my micro-business website
Frontend + 3D for a PixelWar-style community project (>100k users)
Full-stack platform hosting >60 GB of photos with lazy-loaded rendering
Full-stack e-commerce for a client selling lithium boat batteries
Home-automation project (Jarvis-style) with an embedded LLM to control a house
